model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The run is fixed to 256 optimizer steps, so increasing batch size should expose the model to substantially more training examples within the same step budget. A modest learning-rate bump matches the larger batch and keeps the current AdamW+warmup+cosine setup simple.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current optimizer and batching changes helped substantially, which suggests the setup can use a bit more capacity within the same training recipe. Adding one more residual block is a compact architecture change that increases expressiveness without changing tensor shapes or introducing risky optimization behavior.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model is now training more effectively but still under a short fixed budget, so a small amount of label smoothing is a cheap way to improve generalization and calibration without changing optimizer dynamics or runtime. This is a minimal regularization edit with low implementation risk.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After the extra residual block, the current learning rate may be slightly aggressive, and the last regularization change was mildly negative. Backing off to the earlier loss while lowering the step size is a compact way to retune optimization for the deeper network without adding complexity.","secondary_modes":["caching","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The last step moved away from the best setting by lowering the learning rate. Restoring the better optimizer scale and modestly widening only the classifier head tests extra capacity with very low runtime cost, which is a cleaner next architecture probe than scaling the whole convolutional trunk.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The largest gains so far came from using the fixed 256-step budget to process more examples. Pushing batch size to 256 should let the run cover roughly a full epoch within the same step budget, and a proportional learning-rate increase keeps the AdamW schedule aligned with the larger batch.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current recipe is extracting much more value from the fixed-step budget, and the remaining bottleneck is likely model capacity rather than optimization stability. Adding one more same-shape residual block is a targeted way to deepen the feature extractor without disturbing the training setup that is currently working well.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 10","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current configuration is clearly capacity-limited in a productive way: each added residual block has improved validation loss despite higher runtime. A small width increase from 8 to 10 channels is the next compact trunk-capacity step, with substantially lower risk than a larger width jump or a more complex architecture rewrite.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 10","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The width increase cost a lot of runtime for a worse loss, so reverting that is the clean reset. With only 256 optimizer steps, a smaller AdamW second-moment timescale is a plausible win because it lets the optimizer adapt faster in this short-budget regime without changing the now-strong architecture or batch setup.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current trunk and optimization settings are working well, and the last gain came from relaxing regularization rather than changing dynamics. Expanding only the classifier head is a cheap capacity increase that may better exploit the stronger learned features without the runtime cost of widening the whole convolutional stack.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger classifier head was not helpful, so this restores the better-capacity setting. With weight decay already reduced and the run still constrained to 256 steps, the most plausible remaining gain is a slightly more aggressive learning-rate scale to improve how much progress the model makes within the fixed budget.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The move from 4e-3 to 5e-3 only slightly worsened validation loss, which suggests the optimum may be nearby rather than far away. A midpoint learning rate is the cleanest next probe because it preserves the current strong architecture and regularization settings while refining the only lever that recently showed near-boundary behavior.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The biggest gains in this run came from using the fixed 256-step budget to process more examples, and the current learning-rate optimum already moved upward as batch size increased. A moderate step up to batch size 320 is the cleanest remaining way to trade some runtime for more effective data coverage while keeping the rest of the now-strong recipe intact.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The larger batch was slightly worse, so this restores the stronger batch/learning-rate region. Since every reduction in regularization has helped so far, the next clean test is removing weight decay entirely to see whether this still-small model benefits from even less constraint under the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Removing weight decay improved the current best run, which suggests optimization can still be pushed a bit harder. The cleanest next probe is a modest learning-rate increase on top of the stronger zero-weight-decay setting, since the earlier 5e-3 result was obtained under heavier regularization.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The higher learning rate was too aggressive, so this returns to the best scale. With zero weight decay now looking best, the remaining simple optimizer knob is the AdamW second-moment timescale; reducing beta2 may help the optimizer react faster in this very short 256-step regime when regularization is otherwise minimal.","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"            loss.backward()\n","function":"","new":"","old":"","op":"insert_after","source":"","text":"            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)\n"}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The reduced beta2 did not help, so this restores the best-known optimizer moments. For the last shot, gradient clipping is a compact stability tweak that can improve high-learning-rate AdamW updates under zero weight decay without changing architecture, runtime much, or the overall training recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}
